# Integration Checklist: Platform Mechanics Spike

**Purpose**: Validate TACD-001 integration requirements across Claude Code, Codex, source inventory, platform mechanics, downstream handoffs, and no-behavior-change scope boundaries.
**Created**: 2026-06-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the in-scope source surfaces for the named-tool inventory explicitly defined across Claude agents, Codex agents, skills/references, prerequisite scripts, plugin limitation docs, dependency metadata, generated payloads, and tests/evals? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Source Inventory Plan]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec require each audited finding to include enough evidence for integration review: source path, line context, named capability or tool reference, classification, and rationale? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK003 Are no-finding surfaces required to be recorded rather than silently omitted from the inventory? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Runtime Surface]
- [x] CHK004 Are generated/distributed payload occurrences classified separately from source references so TACD-001 does not hand-edit generated payload semantics? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-011, Plan §Source Inventory Plan]

## Runtime Mechanics Coverage

- [x] CHK005 Is the platform-mechanics report required to cover both Claude Code and Codex for installed tools, MCP/app connectors, skills/plugins, and repo-local helpers? [Coverage, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006a, Plan §Platform Mechanics Plan]
- [x] CHK006 Does the runtime-by-capability matrix require every Claude/Codex capability cell to be labeled source-backed, probe-backed, unsupported, unresolved, or environment-specific? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006a, Data Model §Runtime-by-Capability Matrix Cell]
- [x] CHK007 Are probe appendix requirements specific enough to preserve reproducibility while excluding raw runtime inventories, connector lists, local paths, IDs, tokens, and full transcripts? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006b, Spec §FR-015, Research §Publish Sanitized Probe Summaries Only]
- [x] CHK008 Does the spec distinguish Claude Code declared agent/plugin surfaces from connected parent-session capabilities, and Codex bundled templates/metadata from installed runtime surfaces? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016]

## Cross-Runtime Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Is the directive-home recommendation constrained so shared reference plus per-agent pointers is valid only when static pointer coverage and functional eval-plan coverage are defined for both runtimes? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, Research §Directive Home Requires Static Coverage Plus Eval Plan]
- [x] CHK010 Are TACD-004 eval-plan requirements required to cover both Claude and Codex paths for vendor-neutral setup, fallback, installed-capability selection, evidence reporting, and directive application? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009a]
- [x] CHK011 Do the requirements prevent a recommendation that works for one runtime but lacks equivalent evidence or fallback rules for the other runtime? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, Spec §SC-003, Plan §Directive-Home Recommendation Rule]

## Downstream Handoff Quality

- [x] CHK012 Are TACD-002, TACD-003, and TACD-004 handoff responsibilities separated so behavior-changing agent guidance, prerequisite/user-facing messaging, and final enforcement tests remain in later slices? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Downstream Handoff]
- [x] CHK013 Are active-vs-historical allowlist categories complete enough for TACD-004 to block, allow, or review named-tool references without over-banning provenance or fixtures? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Spec §SC-004, Data Model §Allowlist Category]
- [x] CHK014 Are scope boundaries measurable enough to prove TACD-001 did not edit active runtime guidance, prerequisite behavior, public docs messaging, final enforcement tests, plugin versions, or generated payload semantics? [Measurability, Spec §FR-011, Spec §SC-005, Quickstart §Scenario 5]

## Evidence Notes

- The prerequisite command was run with an explicit feature-directory override because the active branch name `tacd-001-platform-mechanics-spike` does not match the upstream numeric feature-branch pattern. This did not require changing TACD-001 requirements or prerequisite behavior.
- Local inventory evidence was sampled with the Plan commands covering named-tool references, capability vocabulary, active Claude/Codex agent files, and generated Claude/Codex payload files.
